author: y4rr
content: Discovered during C3.1 conformance suite work (#843). Both EpochResult and EvalResult are #[non_exhaustive] but had no public constructor usable from outside the crate (E0639 blocks both struct literals and ..Default::default() spread). Fix: added EpochResult::Default impl, EpochResult::new_with_defaults(), and EvalResult::new_with_defaults() to history.rs. These methods are needed by conformance_train.rs and any downstream crate that builds test fixtures. Not a regression — the structs were always non_exhaustive but the conformance suite is the first external consumer that needs to construct them.
